stops.name: Powder Tower
stops.shortDescription: A striking Gothic tower that once served as one of the original 13 city gates. In the 17th century, it was used to store gunpowder, which earned it its modern name.

stops.name: House of the Black Madonna
stops.shortDescription: A unique masterpiece of Czech Cubist architecture, easily identified by the Baroque statue of the Black Madonna on its corner. The building houses a spectacular Cubist cafe on the first floor.

stops.name: Old Town Square
stops.shortDescription: The historic core of Prague, untouched by modern development. The sprawling square is surrounded by stunning, colorful buildings spanning Gothic, Renaissance, and Baroque architectural styles.

stops.name: Prague Astronomical Clock
stops.shortDescription: Installed in 1410, it is the oldest still-operating astronomical clock in the world. Every hour, massive crowds gather to watch the mechanical procession of the Twelve Apostles.

stops.name: Clementinum
stops.shortDescription: A vast complex of historic buildings that once housed a Jesuit college. It is famous for its breathtaking Baroque library hall, complete with intricate frescoes and rare antique globes.

stops.name: Charles Bridge
stops.shortDescription: A historic stone bridge crossing the Vltava river, commissioned by King Charles IV in 1357. Lined with 30 statues of saints, it is one of the most romantic and photographed locations in Europe.

stops.name: Lesser Town Bridge Towers
stops.shortDescription: Guarding the western end of the Charles Bridge, these two distinct, connected towers mark the dramatic entrance into Malá Strana, the Lesser Town.

stops.name: John Lennon Wall
stops.shortDescription: A continuously changing, vibrant wall filled with graffiti, lyrics, and messages of peace. It originated in the 1980s as a form of silent youth protest against the communist regime.

stops.name: Church of St. Nicholas
stops.shortDescription: Widely considered the ultimate masterpiece of Prague Baroque architecture. Its massive green copper dome and adjacent bell tower dominate the skyline of the Lesser Town.

stops.name: Nerudova Street
stops.shortDescription: A picturesque, steep, and winding cobblestone street that formed part of the historic Royal Route. Look up to see the fascinating historic signs and sculpted emblems above the doorways.

stops.name: Prague Castle Gates
stops.shortDescription: The grand entrance to the largest ancient castle complex in the world. Guarded by the imposing 'Battling Titans' statues, it marks the entry into the historic seat of Bohemian kings.

stops.name: St. Vitus Cathedral
stops.shortDescription: A magnificent Gothic cathedral located entirely within the castle walls. It took nearly 600 years to complete and features dazzling stained glass windows designed by Alphonse Mucha.

stops.name: Old Royal Palace
stops.shortDescription: The original seat of Bohemian princes, famous for the massive Vladislav Hall, a spectacular late-Gothic vaulted space that was once used for indoor jousting tournaments.

stops.name: Golden Lane
stops.shortDescription: A charming, narrow street of tiny, brightly colored 16th-century houses built directly into the castle walls. Famous writer Franz Kafka once lived and worked at No. 22.
